criterion: Alt text / accessible naming: informative interactive elements have meaningful names
description: In the main-conversation-trace Timeline SVG, the individual inference/tool segments (elements with `data-seg-idx`) have no accessible name (no aria-label/title exposed to the accessibility tree) even though they are clickable and open a detail drawer. By contrast, the outer waterfall bar and the whole-session graphics-symbol elsewhere in the app DO carry full descriptive labels (e.g. 'main conversation — the chat itself (7.0 s · $0.808). Click to inspect its timeline.'). A keyboard/screen-reader user has no way to discover or distinguish the per-segment click targets inside the timeline.
expected: Each timeline segment should expose an accessible name/label similar to the pattern already used on the waterfall bar and node graphics-symbols, e.g. 'Inference step 1 · 5.9s · click for details' or 'Tool call: Read · 94ms · click for details'.
actual: Segments are plain generic SVG rects with only a `data-seg-idx` data attribute and a `title`-only hover tooltip (per the existing documented gap for cost tooltips in STORY docs) — nothing in the accessibility tree names them individually.
suggested_fix: Add an aria-label (or a <title> child element within the SVG rect, which most screen readers will pick up) to each segment mirroring the hover-tooltip text already computed for mouseover, so the same string serves both sighted-hover and accessibility-tree consumers.
